Condax.pain_point: fragmented forecasting and slow handoff between revenue teams
Condax.proof_point: governed pipeline review with approval-aware follow-up planning

Acme Corporation.priority_context: at-risk account needing tighter GTM coordination
Acme Corporation.pain_point: stalled opportunities and uneven rep follow-through
Acme Corporation.proof_point: bounded risk reviews and explainable next-best actions
Acme Corporation.next_step: a practical walkthrough of its stalled-opportunity posture

Codehow.pain_point: manual scoring and inconsistent routing decisions
Codehow.proof_point: governed scoring and approval-gated routing previews

variants.message: Frame the conversation around pipeline waste reduction before discussing pricing.
variants.rationale: Keeps the conversation on measurable operating value.

variants.message: Offer a bounded pilot focused on one GTM workflow instead of a broad rollout.
variants.rationale: Reduces perceived risk and keeps scope concrete.

variants.message: Position governed service boundaries and auditability as the differentiator.
variants.rationale: Shifts the comparison away from feature checklists toward control and trust.

variants.message: Use the multi-service proof to show predictable composition rather than one opaque agent.
variants.rationale: Shows operational realism instead of generic autonomy claims.

variants.message: Anchor on ANIP in front of existing systems so the buyer does not need a full rebuild.
Use Phase 1 through Phase 4 proof points to show incremental rollout instead of a big-bang launch.
